title: Refactor a broken unique constraint that uses the partial :states list
prompt: The worker in `test_fixtures/broken_unique_states_worker.ex` has a unique config that excludes `:available` from `:states`, which per the Oban docs and multiple Elixir Forum threads can crash the queue. Rewrite the unique block to use the FULL safe state list: `[:available, :scheduled, :executing, :retryable, :completed, :cancelled, :discarded]`. Keep the rest of the module (queue, perform/1) intact. Add a short `@moduledoc` referencing that partial state lists are unsafe. Write to `lib/my_app/workers/conversation_update_worker.ex`.
tier_rationale: Requires knowing that partial state lists that exclude `:available` are documented as unsafe by both the docs and the maintainer. Sonnet rarely catches this without priming.
research_citation: Elixir Forum thread 69648: 'As you can see, the available state was not included… crashed the queue and started creating hundreds of producers for the same queue.'
